title: ferray-core: no ascontiguousarray / asfortranarray equivalents
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy provides np.ascontiguousarray() and np.asfortranarray() which return C-contiguous or F-contiguous copies only when necessary (no-op if already in the right layout). These are used heavily before BLAS calls, SIMD loops, and FFI. ferray-core has no equivalent. Users can check .layout() and manually copy, but there's no single method that says 'give me a contiguous version, copying only if needed'. Add as_standard_layout() -> CowArray (borrows if already C-contiguous, copies otherwise) and as_fortran_layout() -> CowArray. This is a natural use of CowArray.
